message.content: An adversary may use the technique of reverting a cloud instance to its previous state to evade detection and erase evidence of malicious activities. This is typically achieved by restoring virtual machine (VM) or data storage snapshots via the cloud management dashboard or cloud APIs. In highly virtualized environments, such as cloud-based infrastructures, attackers can also exploit temporary storage attached to compute instances, which is often reset upon stopping or restarting the VM. The potential impact includes the loss of forensic evidence and the ability to track malicious actions, complicating incident response efforts. Common exploitation methods involve leveraging cloud provider features for snapshot restoration and temporary storage manipulation. Mitigation strategies include implementing strict access controls, monitoring and logging snapshot and storage activities, and employing continuous monitoring solutions to detect and respond to suspicious activities promptly.
